scenario: A User Task approve-purchase must be visible to multiple users in the candidate group purchasing-managers. Any of them can claim and complete it.
question: Which configuration fits?

options.text: Set the assignee property to a comma-separated list of manager usernames. The assignee field on a Zeebe User Task accepts a single user identifier and assigns the task directly to that one person at activation time. Comma-separated values are not parsed as a list of candidates; the entire string is treated as one user identity, which would fail to match any valid single-user login in Tasklist.

zeebe:assignmentDefinition candidateGroups="purchasing-managers" (static group name or FEEL expression) -- places the task into Tasklist's candidate pool for all members of the group. Any member can see and claim the task; once claimed, that member becomes the exclusive assignee and the task is removed from others' queues. This is the canonical multi-candidate group pattern in Camunda 8, widely used for approval workflows requiring any-of-group completion.

Create five parallel User Tasks -- one explicitly assigned to each purchasing manager. This requires knowing the number and identity of all managers at design time, hard-coding them into the model. It creates five simultaneous approval tasks for a decision that requires only one approval, meaning up to five managers could each independently approve the same purchase, leading to duplicate or conflicting decisions.

Multi-Instance subprocess iterating over a collection of manager IDs. Multi-Instance creates one task instance per element in a list, running them in parallel or sequentially. All instances must complete before the Multi-Instance finishes.

a.text: Incorrect. candidateGroups exposes the task to all group members in Tasklist. First user to claim becomes assignee; others can no longer claim.

c.text: Incorrect. 5 tasks for one piece of work.

d.text: Incorrect. MI runs many instances; here one task should be picked from a pool.
